message.content: {"items":[{"title":"Latin American Public Opinion Project (LAPOP): Uruguay 1965","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA8033","justification":"National survey in Uruguay in the mid‑1960s focused on political attitudes, democratic values, and social behaviour, covering political behaviour and broader social attitudes in a higher‑education relevant urban population."},{"title":"Student Political Attitudes in Uruguay 1967","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA8034","justification":"Survey of university and higher‑education students in Uruguay in 1967 examining political orientations, participation, and attitudes toward institutions, directly linking higher/further education with political behaviour and social attitudes."},{"title":"Political Socialization of University Students in Latin America: Uruguay Study 1968","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA8035","justification":"Study of university students in Uruguay in 1968 regarding political socialization, ideology, and participation, situated in the higher‑education context and focused on political and social attitudes."},{"title":"World Values Survey – Uruguay Pilot 1969","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA8036","justification":"Pilot values study fielded in Uruguay around 1969 measuring social and political values, interpersonal trust, and attitudes toward institutions, fitting the social behaviour and political attitudes themes for the late 1960s."},{"title":"Urban Middle-Class Attitudes in Montevideo 1964","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA8037","justification":"Survey among educated urban residents in Montevideo, including many with higher or further education, focusing on social behaviour, civic engagement, and political opinions in 1964."},{"title":"Youth and Politics in Uruguay 1963","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA8038","justification":"National study of youth including secondary and tertiary students in Uruguay in 1963, examining political participation, party sympathy, and social attitudes toward authority and change."},{"title":"Teachers and Political Change in Uruguay 1966","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA8039","justification":"Survey of school and higher‑education teachers in Uruguay in 1966, focused on their political attitudes, views on educational reform, and broader social values."},{"title":"Social Stratification and Educational Mobility in Uruguay 1962","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA8040","justification":"Study on educational attainment, including higher and further education, and its relation to political orientations and social attitudes among Uruguayans in the early 1960s."},{"title":"Student Movements and Democracy in Uruguay 1969","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA8041","justification":"Dataset on university and further‑education student movements in Uruguay in 1969, analysing protest behaviour, political engagement, and social attitudes in the higher‑education sector."},{"title":"Political Culture in Uruguay 1961","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA8042","justification":"Survey of adult population in Uruguay in 1961 measuring political trust, democratic support, and social behaviour indicators, relevant to political and social attitudes in the 1960s context."}]}
